videos.title: The Patterns Practice Song | Repeating Patterns for Kids
videos.description: A catchy song that introduces repeating patterns using colors, shapes, and numbers. Learn to spot AB, ABC, and AABB patterns all around you!
learningObjectives: Identify repeating patterns in colors, shapes, and numbers
Recognize AB, ABC, and AABB pattern types
keyConceptsCovered: Repeating patterns repeat the same group over and over

videos.title: Pump Up the Pattern | Fun Exercise Pattern Song for Kids
videos.description: Get moving and learn about repeating and growing patterns! Create AB and ABC patterns using body movements like clapping, stomping, and jumping.
learningObjectives: Create repeating patterns using body movements
Distinguish between AB and ABC body patterns
keyConceptsCovered: Patterns can be made with actions, not just shapes

videos.title: Repeating Patterns in Real Life | Patterns Around You
videos.description: Discover repeating patterns in everyday life: floor tiles, fabric designs, wallpaper, fences, and nature. Learn to spot the repeating core in real-world patterns.
learningObjectives: Identify repeating patterns in real-world objects
Find the core (repeating unit) of a pattern
keyConceptsCovered: Patterns appear in tiles, fabric, fences, and nature
The 'core' is the part that repeats
Finding the core helps you extend the pattern

videos.title: What Comes Next? | Repeating Pattern Practice Quiz
videos.description: Test yourself with repeating pattern puzzles! Look at each pattern, find the repeating core, and predict what comes next. Interactive quiz-style video.
learningObjectives: Predict the next item in a repeating pattern
keyConceptsCovered: Finding what comes next by identifying the core
Practice with AB, ABC, ABB, and AABB patterns

videos.title: Create Your Own Repeating Patterns | Pattern Art Activity
videos.description: Use colors, shapes, and objects to create your own repeating patterns. Make pattern bracelets, pattern borders, and pattern art!
learningObjectives: Create original repeating patterns using different media

excellent_performance.description: For students who scored >= 90% on readiness
excellent_performance.rationale: Fast-track through repeating patterns with real-life application and creative challenge

ready_performance.description: For students who scored 80-89% on readiness
ready_performance.rationale: Complete coverage of repeating patterns with practice and creative activity

partial_readiness.description: For students who scored 50-79% on readiness
partial_readiness.rationale: Core repeating pattern concepts after prerequisite review

after_prerequisite_review.description: For students who scored < 50% but completed prerequisites

questions.question: In the pattern: Clap, Stomp, Clap, Stomp, Clap, __ what comes next?
questions.explanation: This is an AB pattern: Clap, Stomp keeps repeating. After Clap, the next action is Stomp.

usageNotes.teacherGuidance: Use real objects (beads, blocks, colored tiles) to create repeating patterns in class. Videos 1-3 are essential for all students. Encourage students to find patterns on their clothes, in the classroom, and outside.
usageNotes.parentGuidance: Help your child spot repeating patterns at home: kitchen tiles, curtain designs, fence rails. Ask 'What is the repeating part?' and 'What comes next?'
usageNotes.studentGuidance: Look around you for patterns! Check your shirt, the floor, and even sounds. Can you find the repeating part and continue the pattern?
